component: W1 representative-frame transport gate (M5): CPS-observable cross-sectional joints (family A, floor-priced), SSA administrative margins of the deployed+simulated benefits (family B, anchor-priced), and the two ordinal compression fingerprints that must reverse to the anchor orderings (family C, binary).
purpose: Gate-W1 pre-lock evidence (v1): step 1 of the lock ceremony (#151 design; #113 M5; #100 W1). Reads no gate, changes no gate, writes NO gates.yaml block -- the gate_w1 stub is proposed in #151 and inserted only by the ratifying flip. It certifies the TRANSPORT of the PSID-estimated generators onto the certified populace frame, NOT the dynamics (2a/2b/2c/M4-certified) nor the projection (M6+).

ceremony.step: 1 of the lock ceremony (floors -> referee -> fixes -> verification -> ratifying flip). Never marked ready/merged by its author.

estimand.claim: Family A gates EXACTLY the CPS-observable cross-sectional cells the certified L0-sparse-refit file carries with verified non-zero support; NOT an uncalibrated CPS extract and NOT the PSID estimation sample. Per governance.description_claims_exactly_the_scored_surface.

cell_families.earnings_participation: weighted share with positive labor earnings, by age band x sex
cell_families.earnings_profile: weighted median earnings / prime-age (35-44, both sexes) median -- the age-earnings profile, by age band x sex
cell_families.earnings_p90p50: within-cell weighted p90/p50 earnings ratio (upper dispersion)
cell_families.earnings_p50p10: within-cell weighted p50/p10 earnings ratio (lower dispersion)
cell_families.marital_share: weighted share in each marital status, by age band x sex
cell_families.hh_size_share: person-level weighted share by household size (1/2/3/4/5+)
cell_families.coresident_spouse: weighted share living with a spouse (A_MARITL spouse-present), by age band x sex

family_a.statistic: |ln(rate_a/rate_b)| on the file's own weighted moments; tolerance round(mean + 4*sd, 3) capped at T_max = ln(1.5).
family_a.split_note: HOUSEHOLD-disjoint (not merely person-disjoint): CPS is a household-cluster sample and the composition moments are household-level, so the honest null keeps every household on one side (the 2c 'couple/person-disjoint where units correlate' lesson).
family_a.scope_note: Family A is INTERNAL transport consistency to the certified frame -- the deployed cross-section must equal the file's own within its sampling floor. The frame's own fidelity to Census/CPS margins is populace-certified, NOT re-litigated here.

internal_noise_floor.method: half_vs_half -- two household-disjoint halves of the certified frame scored against each other; the sampling-noise floor of the DEPLOYMENT frame (not PSID).

protocol.option: a -- the gate-1 / tranche-2a/2b/2c mirror: per-seed HOUSEHOLD-DISJOINT holdout with train-complement fitting, for which the half-vs-half floor is exactly the null. The 4-of-5 seed conjunction is operative.
protocol.estimator: mean-over-K=20-draws (tranche 2a amendment 1, ratified 2026-07-08 PR 96), adopted FROM THE START of this W1 build. rbar_candidate,s is the MEAN over K=20 pre-registered deployment draws (numpy default_rng(9100 + k), k=0..19; a stream DISTINCT from the split seeds), scored ONCE per cell as |ln(rbar / rate_a)| -- NOT the mean of the per-draw |ln| scores.
protocol.statistic: |ln(rbar_candidate,s / rate_a,s)| per cell: rbar the K=20-draw mean of the deployed cell rate on seed s's holdout households, rate_a the holdout half's own weighted rate. Symmetric, scale-free -- the gate-2a/2b/2c statistic.
protocol.varies_per_seed: the 50/50 household-disjoint split (holdout = side A of split_panel_by_person(household_id, seed=s)), the candidate's fit complement, and its K=20 deployed draws of the holdout.
protocol.scored_against: side A's own weighted rate (rate_a), committed per gate seed in noise_floor_per_seed. The half-vs-half floor |ln(rate_A/rate_B)| is exactly the null a faithful transport realises.
protocol.pass_rule: Seed-level conjunction. Seed s passes iff every gate-eligible family-A cell's |ln(rbar/rate_a)| <= its derived tolerance at seed s; the gate passes iff >=4 of 5 gate seeds pass. Report-only family-A cells, all family-B anchors, and the family-C fingerprints are published on their own rules.

faithful_candidate_oc.basis_note: DRAW-NOISE-FREE half-normal basis; ACHIEVABLE under the K=20 mean, UNACHIEVABLE under a single draw. Bound to faithful_candidate_oc by tests/test_gate_w1_derivations.py.

fresh_run_artifact_schema.applies_to: a fresh W1 candidate one-shot run registered AFTER the gate_w1 lock; NEVER this floor build (real-vs-real, no draws).

per_draw_per_cell_rates.rule: commit every draw's per-cell deployed rate r[k, cell, s] for all K=20 draws, all 53 gated cells, all 5 gate seeds, so rbar and |ln(rbar/rate_a)| recompute cell-by-cell.

undefined_draw_rule.rule: if any gated cell's deployed rate is UNDEFINED on any draw, the RUN IS INVALIDATED and must be re-registered; no draw is dropped, skipped, or re-rolled.

per_draw_dispersion_disclosure.note: REPORT-ONLY: the sd across the K=20 draws per gated cell per seed, so a referee sees whether a passing mean conceals a wild draw.

knobs.note: Load-bearing knobs pinned against the module so a mutation is caught WITHOUT the certified-frame reproduction.

weight_concentration.note: The L0-sparse-refit file concentrates weight (a few households carry very large weight), so the effective sample is far below the nominal 166,302 persons. The household-disjoint split keeps each heavy household intact on one side, so a few cells have a HEAVY-TAILED half-split floor whose max over the 100 seeds exceeds mean+4*sd; the W1 heavy-tail guard (report_reason floor_max_exceeds_tolerance) demotes exactly those, guaranteeing a faithful candidate and the training copy pass every gated cell. The honest, self-correcting behaviour, disclosed here.

aggregations_note: EMPTY by design: gate-W1 declares no family-A coverage-recovery aggregates (the 2c stance). Sparse marital/household cells stay report-only by POWER, not masking; a fixes round may add a pooled aggregate only where it is standalone-gateable.

holdout_ids.numpy_generator: populace_dynamics.harness.panel.split_panel_by_person on household_id: side A (holdout) = households h of np.sort(unique household_id) with np.random.default_rng(seed).random(n_households)[h] < 0.5. Every person of a household shares its id, so the holdout is a household-coherent CPS-cluster set; the sha256 is over the holdout household ids.

training_copy_check.candidate: verbatim copy of the train half (side B rates)
training_copy_check.interpretation: A train-copy passes at ~the noise floor, as any moment gate's copy must; the memorisation defence is procedural (registration + holdout exclusion + no_self_rescue), NOT the cell set. What the family-A cells DO catch is a transport that DISTORTS the deployed cross-section: a generator whose deployed earnings x age x sex, marital, or household composition departs from the certified frame's own beyond the CPS sampling floor.

faithful_candidate_oc.method: Independence-approx normal OC (draw-noise-free half-normal basis): a faithful candidate's per-cell score ~ half-normal(realized_sigma); the mean-over-K=20 estimator SHARES that basis, so this OC is ACHIEVABLE (a single-draw estimator would make it UNACHIEVABLE -- tranche-2a amendment-1 finding 1).

draft_thresholds.statistic: abs log ratio of the weighted cell rate
draft_thresholds.note: DRAFT GATE-W1 family-A tolerances -- PRE-LOCK, NOT RATIFIED (v1, step 1 of the lock ceremony). NOT yet mirrored into gates.yaml; the ratifying flip inserts a gate_w1 block. tolerance == round(floor mean + 4*sd, 3), capped at T_max = ln(1.5).

family_b.tolerance_rule: vintage/measurement: tolerance_pp = round(K_VINTAGE * detrended_residual_sd + MEASUREMENT_PP, 2) over the last 10 published vintages; gated iff <= T_max_pp = 3.0. Detrended so the FRA-transition / DI-ageing TREND is disclosed (trend_pp_per_year) and the tolerance prices the year-to-year NOISE, not the trend.

claim_age.age62|male.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age63|male.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age64|male.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age65|male.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age66|male.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.disability_conversion|male.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age67_69|male.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age70plus|male.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age62|female.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age63|female.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age64|female.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age65|female.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age66|female.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.disability_conversion|female.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age67_69|female.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

claim_age.age70plus|female.units: percentage points of the claim-age distribution

di_prevalence.under30.units: percentage points of the disabled-worker age distribution (All disabled workers panel)

di_prevalence.30-34.units: percentage points of the disabled-worker age distribution (All disabled workers panel)

di_prevalence.35-39.units: percentage points of the disabled-worker age distribution (All disabled workers panel)

di_prevalence.40-44.units: percentage points of the disabled-worker age distribution (All disabled workers panel)

di_prevalence.45-49.units: percentage points of the disabled-worker age distribution (All disabled workers panel)

di_prevalence.50-54.units: percentage points of the disabled-worker age distribution (All disabled workers panel)

di_prevalence.55-59.units: percentage points of the disabled-worker age distribution (All disabled workers panel)

di_prevalence.60-fra.units: percentage points of the disabled-worker age distribution (All disabled workers panel)

benefit_level.note: Levels are report-only in v1: they depend on the transported AIME the W1 candidate deploys (not yet built), and only a single 2022 vintage is staged, so no machine vintage sd is derivable. Recorded with an uprating-band context tolerance a fixes round can gate once the AIME transport lands.

family_b.note: The deployed + simulated benefits must land on these admin margins PSID never sees. Point values -> no sampling floor; the tolerance is the anchor's own vintage noise. Benefit LEVELS are report-only until the transported AIME is deployed.

family_c.pricing: binary ordinal (committed anchor orderings; no floor)
family_c.check: on the certified representative frame BOTH fingerprints reverse to required_representative_order (Kendall tau -> 1.0 vs the anchor), and no OTHER adjacency changes. If transport is real both reverse; if cosmetic they do not.

c1.name: PPI<->NRA at the PIA bends
c1.mechanism: a representative frame carries more AIME above the second PIA bend, where progressive price indexing bites, so PPI's savings exceed NRA->70's -- reversing the compressed-career order.

c2.name: elimination<->+2pp at the taxable maximum
c2.mechanism: a representative frame carries >16.1% of payroll above the wage base (the break-even), so taxable-max elimination's revenue gain exceeds +2pp's -- reversing the compressed-tail order.

warts.wart: The certified default is the L0-sparse-refit-57k file, which zeroes UNTARGETED inputs. Family A builds moments only on columns verified populated (age, is_female, employment_income_before_lsr, A_MARITL, household membership; assert_columns_populated fails loudly otherwise). Cells on a zeroed column are OUT of scope by construction, not silently gated.

Household weights are highly concentrated (max ~433k vs mean ~2.3k), so the Kish effective n is far below the nominal sample. The household-disjoint split keeps heavy households intact, so a few cells have a heavy-tailed half-split floor. Unlike the light-tailed PSID floors of 2a/2b/2c, a mean+4*sd tolerance does not always cover the worst seed, so a NEW gate-eligibility criterion (the heavy-tail guard: tolerance >= floor max over the 100 seeds, report_reason floor_max_exceeds_tolerance) demotes exactly those cells; k stays 4 (the precedent). This guarantees the training copy passes 5/5 at <=1x (as in 2a/2b/2c). Disclosed in weight_concentration + the partition reasons.

Family A checks the deployed cross-section against the certified FRAME'S OWN moments, not against Census/CPS external truth. The frame's fidelity to admin margins is populace's calibration certification, OUTSIDE W1. W1-A is transport-fidelity to the deployment frame.

The claim-age distribution (FRA transition) and DI age composition (workforce ageing) TREND. The vintage tolerance is the DETRENDED residual sd (year-to-year noise), and the trend is disclosed per category (trend_pp_per_year); categories whose detrended tolerance still exceeds T_max_pp demote to report-only. No trend is hidden inside a wide tolerance.

Benefit LEVELS (6.B4 PIA / 6.B3 MOB) are report-only: they depend on the transported AIME the W1 candidate deploys (not yet built), and only a 2022 vintage is staged (no machine vintage sd). Recorded with an uprating-band context, gate-able by a fixes round once the AIME transport lands.

This floor RECORDS the two committed before/after orderings; it does NOT run them on a representative frame (the transport generator does not exist yet -- it is the eventual candidate). The binary reversal is checked at candidate time; here it is the pre-committed anchor the gate will bind.

Family B anchors the DI age COMPOSITION (Table 19), not the prevalence RATE: the insured denominator (Supplement 4.C2) is not staged (#123 wanted-list). The composition is gated; the rate is deferred, named, not fabricated.

Family-A earnings = employment + self-employment labor income BEFORE the labor-supply response (the certified input columns), the CPS-observable analog of the gate-1 PSID labor-income concept. Not the post-LSR modelled earnings; disclosed.

draft_thresholds_note: DRAFT GATE-W1 VALIDATION EVIDENCE -- PRE-LOCK, NOT RATIFIED (v1, step 1 of the gate-W1 lock ceremony; the 2b/2c round lessons pre-empted). The lock ceremony (referee -> fixes -> verification -> ratifying flip) controls the lock; this is a DRAFT, never marked ready or merged by its author.

revision_pins.build_env: policyengine.py .venv (pandas 2.3.2 / pytables 3.11.1 / huggingface_hub 0.34.4) with populace_dynamics on PYTHONPATH; the certified pin comes from pe.us.model.release_bundle (policyengine 4.18.8), resolved by revision+sha256.
revision_pins.build_commit_note: gate-w1-floors v1: certified_artifact_sha256 pins the deployment frame; no PSID and no populace-fit.
